original: US Voyager I takes 1st space photograph of Earth & Moon together
Uğur Şahin, German immunologist (BioNTech - created 1st mRNA-based vaccine for COVID-19), born in İskenderun, Turkey
Ray Washburn (Cards) no-hits SF Giants 2-0
George Meegen completes 2,426d (19K mi) walk across Western Hemisphere
Intrepid (US) beats Dame Pattie (Australia) in 21st America's Cup yacht race at Newport, Rhode Island
500 lb 6' Hershey Kiss is displayed at 1 Times Square, NYC
Mel Brooksand Buck Henry's spy spoof sitcom "Get Smart" starringDon Adams, Barbara Feldon, and Edward Platt, debuts on NBC-TV
June Foray, American voice actress (Rocky the Flying Squirrel; Cinderella; Cindy Lou Who), born in Springfield, Massachusetts (d. 2017)
Valentina Ponomaryova, Soviet cosmonaut (Vostok 6 backup), born in Moscow, Russia
Baseball major league record 4 grand slams hit
Soyuz 38 carries 2 cosmonauts (1 Cuban) to Salyut 6 space station
Joe Kittinger completes 1st solo balloon crossing of Atlantic
Juan de la Cierva flies 1st helicopter above Channel
BBC Radio reports prime # 2^216091-1 found in Houston
Vanguard 3 launched into Earth orbit
TV sitcom series "The Addams Family", starringJohn Astin,Carolyn Jones,Jackie Coogan, andTed Cassidypremieres on ABC; runs for two seasons
Momofuku Andomarkets the first Cup Noodle, packaging it in a waterproof polystyrene container
The Seneca tribe gives Harriet Maxwell Converse an honorary position as chief, making her the first white woman to become a Native American chief
Chile declares independence from Spain, celebrated today as Chile's National Day
Baseball rules 1st baseman can tag base for out instead of runner
KTUL TV channel 8 in Tulsa, OK (ABC) begins broadcasting
Hurricane Hugo causes extensive damage in Puerto Rico
LA Mighty Ducks play their 1st NHL pre-season game against Penguins
Roman EmperorConstantine the Greatdecisively defeats rival Emperor Licinius at Chrysopolis
Scott Joplingranted copyright for his "Maple Leaf Rag", the most famous ragtime composition, by the US Copyright Office
TV sitcom "I Dream of Jeannie", starringBarbara Edenas a 2,000-year-old genie, andLarry Hagmanas an astronaut who becomes her master, premieres on NBC
Ken Burn's documentary miniseries "Baseball" premieres on PBS (Emmy for Outstanding Informational Series)
"Rush Hour" directed by Brett Ratner, starringJackie ChanandChris Tuckerreleased - Jackie Chan's Hollywood breakthrough

Francesca Caccini, Italian singer, composer (La liberazione di Ruggiero - widely believed to be oldest opera from a female composer), and teacher, born in Florence, Grand Duchy of Tuscany (d. after 1641)
Henriette Renié, French harp prodigy, composer, and teacher, born in Paris, France (d. 1956)
Nikolay Rukavishnikov, Soviet cosmonaut (Soyuz 10, 16, 33), born in Tomsk, Russia (d. 2002)
Ralph Backstrom, Canadian ice hockey centre (6 x Stanley Cup Montreal Canadiens; 6 x NHL All-Star), born in Kirkland Lake, Ontario (d. 2021)
Charles Lacy Veach, American astronaut (STS 39, STS 52), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 1995)
Dee Dee Ramone [Douglas Colvin], American punk rock bassist (Ramones. 1974-89 - "I Wanna Be Sedated"), born in Fort Lee (now Fort Gregg-Adams), Virginia (d. 2002)
Toni Kukoč, Croatian basketball forward (NBA C'ship 1996, 97, 98 Chicago Bulls; NBA Sixth Man of the Year 1996; 5 × Euroscar Player of the Year), born in Split, Croatia
Britain's first twin-engine airplane (Short S.39) is test flown
WWII: Eindhoven, Netherlands freed by American and British troops (Lightly Day)
"The Day the Earth Stood Still", science fiction film directed by Robert Wise, and starring Michael Rennie, opens

Space shuttle STS 48 (Discovery 14) lands
US PresidentGeorge Washingtonlays the cornerstone of the Capitol Building in Washington, D.C.
Justinus Kerner, German physician and medical writer (first detailed description of botulism), born in Ludwigsburg, Baden-Württemberg, Germany (d. 1862)
Grey Owl [Archibald Belaney], Canadian pioneering conservationist (who pretended to be part Native American), born in Hastings, England (d. 1938)[1]
Agnes De Mille, American dancer and choreographer (Oklahoma!), born in New York City (d. 1993)
Syd Howe, Canadian Hockey HOF left wing (Stanley Cup 1936, 37, 43 Detroit Red Wings), born in Ottawa, Ontario (d. 1976)
Johnny Mantz, American auto racer (Southern 500, 1950, first 500-mile race in NASCAR history), born in Hebron, Indiana (d. 1972)
Bertha Wilson, Canadian lawyer and the 1st woman appointed to the Supreme Court of Canada, born in Kirkcaldy, Scotland (d. 2007)
June Fisher [June Thorndycraft], British teacher and trade unionist who developed CSE qualification and GCSE exams, born in Chatham, England (d. 1995)
Valery Ryumin, Soviet cosmonaut (Soyuz 25, 32/34, 35/37), born in Komsomolsk-on-Amur, Russia
Takao Doi, Japanese astronaut (STS 87), born in Tokyo, Japan
Billy Sims, American College Football Hall of Fame running back (Heisman Trophy 1978, U of Oklahoma; Pro Bowl 1980–82, Detroit Lions), born in St. Louis, Missouri
John Powell, British film score composer, based in America (Happy Feet; Bourne films), born in London, England
Holly Robinson, American actress (21 Jump Street), born in Mount Airy, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania

Fort Ticonderoga in New York opens
